topic: low acidity or structure and flat wine perception
trigger_keywords: qué puede provocar la percepción de un vino

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A wine has insufficient acidity or too little structural definition for its fruit, alcohol, sweetness, and body.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Acidity supplies freshness and tension, while structural components such as acid, tannin, and body give the palate shape and progression.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: When these balancing elements are insufficient, the wine may seem flat, dull, or poorly defined rather than fresh and persistent.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Insufficient acidity or structure can make a wine seem flat because it lacks freshness, tension, or palate definition.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
